upload.title: Upload Your Contract
upload.hero_body: Upload a Japanese contract — photo, PDF, or text. AI analyzes each clause for risk and delivers improvement suggestions in your language.
upload.flow_review_desc: Each clause checked against relevant laws

pii.warning: Personal information detected. Please review before submitting.

pricing.title: Pricing
pricing.quote_exact_desc: This amount is based on text extracted before payment.
pricing.quote_estimated_desc: Image and scanned PDF files are finalized after formal OCR runs post-payment.
pricing.assurance_privacy_desc: Your contract is deleted from the server immediately after analysis
pricing.assurance_delivery_desc: Report link sent to your email as soon as analysis completes

payment.title: Payment
payment.waiting_note: Processing payment. Please do not close this page.
payment.success_note: Payment successful. Analysis will begin shortly.

review.title: Analyzing
review.tool_analyze_clause_risk: Checking relevant laws and risk grounds...
review.phase_parsing_desc: Reading the contract structure and clauses...
review.phase_analyzing_desc: Analyzing each clause against relevant laws...
review.phase_generating_desc: Compiling analysis results into a clear report...
review.reconnect_failed: Unable to reconnect. Please tap the button below to try again.

report.title: Analysis Report
report.expires_in: This report will be automatically deleted in {{hours}} hours
report.share_text: I analyzed my contract with ContractGuard
report.language_locked_note: This report was generated in {{language}}. Changing the site language only updates the interface, not the report content itself.
report.share_note: Sharing only sends the report link. The original contract text is never included, and the link expires within 24 hours.
report.original_contract_session_note: Visible only in the current session on this device so you can compare it with the analysis. The server has already deleted the original contract text.
report.original_contract_shared_note: Visible only in the current session on the device that uploaded the contract. For privacy reasons, shared links and emailed links never include the original contract text.
report.comparison_hint: Compare with the original clause inside each analysis card

errors.review_failed: An error occurred during analysis. Please try again.

footer.disclaimer: 本サービスは法律相談ではありません。具体的な法的判断は弁護士にご相談ください (This service is not legal advice. Please consult a licensed attorney for specific legal decisions.)

privacy.summary: ContractGuard puts your privacy first. Contract text is deleted from the server immediately after analysis — we never store it. We only collect your email address, used solely to send the report link.

terms.summary: ContractGuard is an AI-powered contract risk analysis service. It is not legal advice — please consult a licensed attorney for binding legal decisions. No refunds after analysis begins. Reports are automatically deleted after 24 hours.

examples.section_desc: Explore AI analysis examples based on real contract scenarios
examples.rental_c1_reason: This clause requires the tenant to bear all restoration costs including natural wear and tear. Under the Civil Code and MLIT guidelines, normal wear and aging should be borne by the landlord — this clause may be unfair to the tenant.
examples.rental_c1_suggestion: Suggest amending to 'Tenant shall restore only damage caused by intent or negligence,' explicitly excluding natural wear and tear.
examples.rental_c2_reason: A 6-month notice period and 3 months' rent as penalty, while not unheard of in Japan's rental market, exceed the typical standard of 1–2 months. These terms are strict — consider negotiating.
examples.rental_c2_suggestion: Suggest negotiating the notice period down to 2 months and the penalty to 1 month's rent, which is closer to market norms.
examples.rental_c3_reason: A renewal fee of 1 month's rent is common in the Tokyo area, and the Supreme Court has upheld reasonable renewal fee clauses. This clause is within a reasonable range.
examples.rental_c3_suggestion: This is standard practice, but confirm before signing whether the fee applies to every renewal cycle.
examples.employment_c1_reason: A 2-year non-compete period is long, and covering all competitors nationwide is overly broad. Japanese courts tend to void non-compete clauses exceeding 1 year or lacking compensation.
examples.employment_c1_suggestion: Suggest negotiating to 6 months–1 year, limiting the geographic and business scope, and requesting non-compete compensation from the employer.
examples.employment_c2_reason: A 6-month probation is within the legal range (typically 3–6 months), but 'lacking aptitude' as a dismissal criterion is vague, giving the employer wide discretion.
examples.employment_c2_suggestion: Suggest requesting specific criteria for 'lacking aptitude' and confirming whether 30-day advance notice is required for dismissal during probation.
examples.parttime_c1_reason: Paying overtime premium only after 40 hours/month violates the Labor Standards Act. All hours exceeding statutory limits (8h/day, 40h/week) require at least 25% premium pay — there is no 40-hour free overtime exception.
examples.parttime_c1_suggestion: This clause is illegal. Demand it be amended to: 'Overtime premium of at least 25% shall be paid for all hours exceeding statutory working hours.'
examples.parttime_c2_reason: Requiring employees to compensate full damages is typically limited by courts, which usually award only partial liability. Also, deducting from wages requires the worker's written consent — otherwise it violates the full wage payment principle.
examples.parttime_c2_suggestion: Confirm whether the company has business liability insurance, and request that 'wage deductions require the employee's written consent' be explicitly stated.
